desc: Only NumPy's RNG is seeded; Python's random module remains unseeded, so data size, categories, scaling, transformations, and ordering cannot be reproduced from RANDOM_SEED.
Deriving the NumPy seed from int(time.time()) % 1000 provides only 1,000 seeds and causes frequent identical random sequences across separate runs.
mysterious_transform randomly changes values to absolute values before filtering, so identical input data can produce materially different output without any explicit parameter.
aggregate_but_confusing randomly selects both the sort column and direction, making identical aggregated data appear in unpredictable orders.
Aggregating flag with count reports only non-null flag values rather than group row counts, so groups containing missing flags are undercounted.
mysterious_transform adds value_squared and may overwrite value on the caller's DataFrame before returning a filtered DataFrame, creating surprising partial mutation of the input.
Unconditionally calling plt.show() can block execution or fail in headless and automated environments.
